old_claim.note: Experimental Controls provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: The 1753 Committee of Papers governed publication decisions; experimental-control design was neither a committee mechanism nor an institutional prerequisite.

source_shape.source_locator: The Committee decisions section describes the 1753 voting procedure and optional expert referral as the Society's first statutory provision for peer review.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The Royal Society account defines a publication-governance process based on journal and society institutions, not experimental-control technique.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The corrected scope leaves this relationship outside the causal boundary. The 1753 Committee of Papers governed publication decisions; experimental-control design was neither a committee mechanism nor an institutional prerequisite.

ontology_before: scientific_peer_review_practices treated experimental_controls as a direct enabling relationship.
ontology_after: The 1753 Committee of Papers governed publication decisions; experimental-control design was neither a committee mechanism nor an institutional prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: scientific_peer_review_practices no longer has experimental_controls as a direct prerequisite.
would_reject_if: A direct historical or technical source establishes experimental_controls as a necessary causal input to the newly scoped scientific_peer_review_practices claim.
short_reason: The 1753 Committee of Papers governed publication decisions; experimental-control design was neither a committee mechanism nor an institutional prerequisite.
